titleStmt.title: SII 32, part 1, no. 24: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 24. (A.R. No. 176 of 1930.) PIḶḶAIPPĀKKAM, SRIPERUMBUDUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a slab lying in front of the ruined Perumāḷ temple.

This inscription along with the other one found at Tiruniṉṟavūr, a village in the above taluk and district, throw welcome light on the local administration of (Tiru)niṉṟavūr during the time of Parāntaka I.
This highly damaged inscription is in two pieces. At the commencement of the tenth line onwards, a few letters are missing consistently as the slab is broken at the proper left.

It registers a regulation made by the assembly consisting of the great men (perumakkaḷ) of the sabhā of Niṉṟavūr in Palkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam, the great men representing the wards (kuḍumbināl maṉṟāḍugira perumakkaḷ) of the village and a certain officer named Pakūr-Tiruvaḍi, probably representing the crown. The clauses of this regulation which are pieced together from the extant portion of the record may be summed up as follows:
1. With effect from the 20th regnal year (927 A.D.) two persons (pēr) who had no previous experience (ariyādār) as members of the assembly shall represent each ward. (kuḍumbil).
2. That the taxes on lands shall thence-forward be paid through the wards (kuḍumbin vaḻīyē iṟai iṟuppadāgavum).
3. It is also stated here that the lands on which taxes had not been paid shall be sold and the amount due on them realized.
4. That persons acting against the stipulated conditions of this resolution shall be liable to a daily fine one mañjāḍi of gold and the fine so imposed shall be duly collected.
5. The remuneration for the services rendered by the madhyastha shall be given at the rate of one kaḻañju of gold per month and cloth (kūṟai) every six months.
6. That the two of the great men (perumakkaḷ) and one another individual (oruvanukku) shall be paid half kaḻañju per month and a cloth every six months (as in clause 5).
Published in Indian History and Epigraphy Dr. G.S. Gai felicitation volume (1990) pp. 80-81.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī .
matirai koṇṭako
pparakecaripanmaṟku yā
ṇṭu 10 9 Āvatu palku
ṉṟak koṭṭattu niṉṟa
vūr sabhai perumakkaḷum ku
ṭumpiṉāl maṉṟāṭukiṉṟa pe
rumakkaḷum nampān teyva
teyākiya r tiruvaṭimāruṅ
kūṭi Iruntu ceyta javastha
tuRead javasthayāvatuIrupatāvatu mutalāka paṇ
ṉapaṭi Aṟiyātār kuṭumpil
Iraṇṭu per maṉṟāṭu
vumDo maṉṟāṭuvatākavum paṇṇiṉa maṇi
kkuṭumpiṉ vaḻiye Iṟai I
tākavumDo maṉṟāṭuvatākavumkuṭumpil Iṟai
bhumi pākaṅ koḷpatāka
ṉṟāṭum veṇṭi
p per maṉṟāṭuvāṉ
tuku povārākavum va
sabhaiyārey
�rākavum puṟappova
�r nicati mañcāṭi poṉ
ṭam paṭuvatākavum ni
ya taṇṭam taṇṭu
tom Ākavum taṇṭat
ye taṇṭippār Ākavu
kuṟṟa muṟaiyāṉa kuṟṟattukku

Ivve
makkaḷukku
piramāṇañ cetuviṭu
madhyastaṉ
yāṉākavum
[madhya] staṉuk ke ka
ḻañcum Āṟu tiṅkaḷ kūṟaiyu
m Iraṇṭu poṉṉum kuṭuppatāka
vum perumakkaḷ vāritaperuma
Oruvanukkumāka muvarkku
m tiṅkaḷ kaḻañcaraiyum Āka
tiṅkaḷ kūṟaiyum Iraṇṭu po
ṉṉum kuṭuppatākavum pā
m tiruvaṭiyārkkup paṇi cey
tiruvaṭi ṉāṉa kaṇakku
piramāṇa ceytu viṭṭa
Erivāriyap perumakkaḷum
toṭṭavāriya perumakkaḷum
vāriya perumakka
ḷu poṉ vāriyap peru
pa ṭa peru
Damaged.
Digital edition of SII 32, part 1, no. 24 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
